en.value:
-        Train nursing staff to become Skill Birth Attendants (SBA) in Birthing centers (8 weeks residential training at Zonal Hospital Dhangadhi)

	-        Onsite coaching to SBA working at low delivery volume sites to retain their skills (using SBA trainers/or deputation at SZH)

	-        Supporting Strengthening Comprehensive Emergency Obstetric Care (CEOC) site in District Hospital in Martadi with equipment, instruments and training )

	-        Support Strengthening Basic Emergency Obstetric Care (BEOC) site in Kolti, PHC with equipment, instruments and training)

	-        Birthing Center construction and renovation support in two VDC’s Equipment and infrastructure support to 21 birthing centers

	-        Support to establish Solar heating system at birthing center

	-        Support to establish baby corner for neonates in high altitude VDC’s (baby cots, mattress, blankets and lights etc

	-        Implementation of Self Applied techniques for quality Health (SATH) method at community level

	-        Orientation of HWs, FCHVs and HFOMC members on SATH

	-        Orientation and on site coaching to MG meeting on MNH issues

	-        Regular joint monitoring of CEOC, BEOC sites and Birthing centers

	-        Orientation to FCHVs on MNH issues (2 days)

	-        Intensive monitoring of the MNH program

	-        Birth preparedness Package training to FCHVs (2+1 days)

	-        Resource Person for FCHVs training (1 local and 1 from DHO)

	-        Sensitization of MGs, CGSs, VSLA groups on MNH issues to increase service utilization at community level (1day)

	-        Implementation of health exhibition program focusing on MNH issues at low service utilization areas of the district

	-        Display of hoarding boards, Wall painting on MNH issues in high movement areas

	-        Implement the Community Health Score Board approach in Birthing centers

	-        Orientation to DHO staff on Community Health Score Board (CHSB)

	-        Identify the HF for the implementation of CHSB program based in current service statics (good, average, poor performing in terms of MNH program)

	-        Bi-annual review and monitoring of CHSB progress

	-        Orientation to HFOMC members, community leaders including traditional healers on MNH issues

	-        Establish and contribute to district level quality support funds for need based support at HF

	-        Infection prevention training to health facility staff in 21 birthing centers, 4 days per HF plus 2 days follow ups

	-        Peer education approach for MNH awareness in hard to reach communities/including training to Peer educators on MNH and interpersonal communication

	-        Orientation of mother in law, father in law, husband of pregnant women on MNH

	-        Orientation to HFOMC members, community leaders including traditional healers on MNH issues

	-        Development and airing of messages in MNH for Radio and local cable network and airing from local FM stations and local cable channel distributers

	-        Design, develop and distribute IEC/BCC materials

	-        Training to Social Mobilizers on MNH issues (3 days)

	-        Strengthen maternal and new born death audit at district and community level and sharing back to community

	-        Implementation of Self Applied Techniques for quality health (SATH) method at community level (40 sites within 27 VDCs)

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
